sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 3-week history of right shoulder pain, which started after engaging in various yard work projects.
The pain is localized to the outside of the right shoulder, exacerbated by movement, and is more pronounced when the arm is raised.
The patient has a history of similar pain in the same shoulder from a past injury, but this episode is more persistent.
The pain is dull and tender to touch, and the patient experiences some pain at night due to sleeping on that side.
The patient has tried taking Advil and icing the area, which provides some relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of past injury to the right shoulder from playing baseball, but no other relevant medical history is mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness in the arm or hands.
Current medications: The patient is taking Advil for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are within normal limits, including a temperature of 97.8, heart rate of 62, respirations of 16, blood pressure of 116/84, and oxygen saturation of 99%.
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in the right shoulder, with tenderness at the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
The patient has a good hand grip and normal neurovascular exam results, including capillary refill less than 3 seconds and intact sensation to touch.
Test Results: The patient's shoulder x-ray shows no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, which means the tendons of the muscles that help make the shoulder muscles have been injured.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; no other problems mentioned.
Plan:
I referred the patient to physical therapy for approximately 6-8 weeks and advised them to continue taking Tylenol during that time.
If symptoms do not improve, a steroid injection may be considered to provide relief.
I also recommended that the patient consider soaking their feet in Epsom salts to help relieve soreness and stiffness in their feet and ankles.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again after the physical therapy referral.
